name: Kamadhenu

visual_attributes.morphology: A white cow with a female head and breasts, bird wings, and a peacock's tail
distinctive_features: Various deities depicted within her body
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Cow body with human head and bird-like appendages

domain_specific_visual.SYMBOLIC_ELEMENTS: Horns representing Trimurti, eyes as sun and moon

functional_attributes.primary_function: Provider of all desires and prosperity
secondary_functions: Source of milk and milk products

domain_specific_functional.RITUAL_IMPORTANCE: Provides milk and ghee for rituals

relationships.description: Kamadhenu is described as the daughter of Daksha in some texts.
Kamadhenu is described as the wife of the sage Kashyapa.
Nandini is described as the daughter of Kamadhenu.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbol of abundance, fertility, and divine providence

contrastive_constraints: NOT without human head or bird features
NOT solely a deity without bovine features
